{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager",
  "description": "Bayshore Technologies Limited is committed to delivering innovative power and energy solutions across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager
Job Location: Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Business Development / Sales

Job Summary


	We are seeking a results-driven Business Development manager to drive business growth, build strong client relationships, and expand our market presence within the energy and power sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and markets.
	Develop and implement effective business development strategies.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and stakeholders.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis.
	Prepare business proposals and presentations.
	Negotiate and close business deals to achieve revenue targets.
	Collaborate with marketing and sales teams to generate leads.
	Maintain records of sales activities and client interactions.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Sales, or related fields.
	5 year and above proven experience in business development or sales.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM software.
	Experience in the Energy and Power Industry is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: bayshoretechnologies4@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development Manager Job at Bayshore Technologies Limited




        
    

        
        Bayshore Technologies Limited is committed to delivering innovative power and energy solutions across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager
Job Location: Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Business Development / Sales

Job Summary


	We are seeking a results-driven Business Development manager to drive business growth, build strong client relationships, and expand our market presence within the energy and power sector.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and markets.
	Develop and implement effective business development strategies.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and stakeholders.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis.
	Prepare business proposals and presentations.
	Negotiate and close business deals to achieve revenue targets.
	Collaborate with marketing and sales teams to generate leads.
	Maintain records of sales activities and client interactions.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Sales, or related fields.
	5 year and above proven experience in business development or sales.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM software.
	Experience in the Energy and Power Industry is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Painter",
  "description": "Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Painter
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Inspect and assess surfaces to determine appropriate preparation methods, including removal of mold, stains, and repair of damaged areas.
	Prepare surfaces for painting by filling cracks and holes, sanding, and applying primers and sealers to ensure optimal finish and durability.
	Execute interior and exterior painting works, including fa&ccedil;ade applications, using ladders and scaffolding where required.
	Apply paints, putty, and specialized coatings with precision, including spray painting across surfaces such as walls, wood, metal, doors, windows, and ceilings.
	Carry out surface preparation and finishing techniques including POP works, screeding, and gloss applications to achieve high-quality results.
	Protect surrounding areas by covering furniture, floors, and fixtures with appropriate materials such as nylon sheets and masking tape.
	Demonstrate strong communication, integrity, accountability, teamwork, and safety awareness in all assigned tasks.


Relevant work experience / Qualification


	Hold at least an HND/OND qualification or a relevant Trade Test certification.
	3-5 years of experience in painting and surface preparation, preferably with 5-8 years of professional experience.
	Ability to mix and apply several types of paint, including Oil paint, Emulsion Paint, Enamel Paint, Bituminous Paint, Aluminium Paint, Anti-Corrosive Paint, Synthetic Rubber Paint, Cement Paint, etc.
	Strong attention to detail, excellent time management skills, and the ability to work independently or as part of a team.


What We Offer


	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave/Allowance
	Overtime.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment3@ekomaintenance.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Painter Job at Eko Maintenance Limited




        
    

        
        Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Painter
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Inspect and assess surfaces to determine appropriate preparation methods, including removal of mold, stains, and repair of damaged areas.
	Prepare surfaces for painting by filling cracks and holes, sanding, and applying primers and sealers to ensure optimal finish and durability.
	Execute interior and exterior painting works, including façade applications, using ladders and scaffolding where required.
	Apply paints, putty, and specialized coatings with precision, including spray painting across surfaces such as walls, wood, metal, doors, windows, and ceilings.
	Carry out surface preparation and finishing techniques including POP works, screeding, and gloss applications to achieve high-quality results.
	Protect surrounding areas by covering furniture, floors, and fixtures with appropriate materials such as nylon sheets and masking tape.
	Demonstrate strong communication, integrity, accountability, teamwork, and safety awareness in all assigned tasks.


Relevant work experience / Qualification


	Hold at least an HND/OND qualification or a relevant Trade Test certification.
	3-5 years of experience in painting and surface preparation, preferably with 5-8 years of professional experience.
	Ability to mix and apply several types of paint, including Oil paint, Emulsion Paint, Enamel Paint, Bituminous Paint, Aluminium Paint, Anti-Corrosive Paint, Synthetic Rubber Paint, Cement Paint, etc.
	Strong attention to detail, excellent time management skills, and the ability to work independently or as part of a team.


What We Offer


	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave/Allowance
	Overtime.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Graphics Designer",
  "description": "The SRS Collection stands as a beacon of premier luxury, health, and wellness hospitality, celebrated for crafting bespoke wellness and lifestyle retreats tailored to discerning business and leisure travelers alike. Withillustrious properties nestled in central Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphics Designer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Graphics Designer to produce visually engaging designs that align with our brand identity.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for creating high-quality graphics for digital and print platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Design creative assets for social media, marketing campaigns, and internal communications
	Develop brand-aligned visuals, including flyers, brochures, banners, and presentations
	Collaborate with marketing and other teams to deliver compelling design solutions
	Edit images, videos, and other multimedia content as needed
	Ensure consistency in design across all platforms
	Stay updated with design trends and tools


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Graphics Designer or similar role
	Proficiency in design tools such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, and Canva
	Strong creativity and attention to detail
	Good communication and time management skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: srsrecruitment2025@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "description": "Workforce Group is a well-known consulting firm that has several subsidiaries and offers various services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Sales / Business Development Lead

Job Location: Lagos (Remote - Occasional Onsite Engagements)
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Leadership Development / Innovation / Consulting / Ecosystem Development

Description


	We are hiring a high-performing and commercially driven Sales &amp; Business Development Leader to spearhead enterprise growth, strategic partnerships, and revenue expansion for a fast-growing, impact-focused organisation operating at the intersection of leadership development, innovation, and ecosystem building.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and drive enterprise sales across leadership programs, platforms, and events
	Lead high-value B2B sales engagements with senior executives in multinational organisations
	Develop and execute scalable sales strategies to achieve aggressive revenue targets
	Build and manage strategic relationships with HR, L&amp;D, and C-suite stakeholders
	Drive renewals, expansions, and multi-year enterprise partnerships
	Structure and sell bundled offerings, sponsorships, and customised solutions
	Manage sales pipelines, external partners, and agencies to ensure consistent deal flow.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree
	7+ years of experience in business development, enterprise sales, or strategic partnerships
	MBA or Master&rsquo;s degree is an advantage
	Proven track record in selling to senior decision-makers in large organisations
	Experience delivering leadership, consulting, events, or ecosystem-based solutions
	Strong commercial acumen with responsibility for significant revenue generation (?500M+)
	Excellent stakeholder management and negotiation skills
	Strong network across corporate, tech, creative, or institutional sectors is a plus.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: executivesearch@workforcegroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Head of Sales / Business Development Lead Job at Workforce Group




        
    

        
        Workforce Group is a well-known consulting firm that has several subsidiaries and offers various services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Sales / Business Development Lead

Job Location: Lagos (Remote - Occasional Onsite Engagements)
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Leadership Development / Innovation / Consulting / Ecosystem Development

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are hiring a high-performing and commercially driven Sales & Business Development Leader to spearhead enterprise growth, strategic partnerships, and revenue expansion for a fast-growing, impact-focused organisation operating at the intersection of leadership development, innovation, and ecosystem building.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and drive enterprise sales across leadership programs, platforms, and events
	Lead high-value B2B sales engagements with senior executives in multinational organisations
	Develop and execute scalable sales strategies to achieve aggressive revenue targets
	Build and manage strategic relationships with HR, L&D, and C-suite stakeholders
	Drive renewals, expansions, and multi-year enterprise partnerships
	Structure and sell bundled offerings, sponsorships, and customised solutions
	Manage sales pipelines, external partners, and agencies to ensure consistent deal flow.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree
	7+ years of experience in business development, enterprise sales, or strategic partnerships
	MBA or Master’s degree is an advantage
	Proven track record in selling to senior decision-makers in large organisations
	Experience delivering leadership, consulting, events, or ecosystem-based solutions
	Strong commercial acumen with responsibility for significant revenue generation (?500M+)
	Excellent stakeholder management and negotiation skills
	Strong network across corporate, tech, creative, or institutional sectors is a plus.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Maintenance Officer / Technician",
  "description": "Rainoil Limited is an integrated downstream oil and gas company. A prominent player in the Nigerian industry, the company&rsquo;s operations span across the downstream value chain i.e. Petroleum Product Storage, Haulage/Distribution and Retail Sales. Our primary products include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO) and Kerosene (DPK).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Officer / Technician

Job Location: Nigeria
Job Type: Full-time

Description


	Responsible for performing preventive, corrective and routine maintenance. Troubleshooting and repairs on machinery, equipment, to prevent or reduce operational downtime and ensure optimal performance and safety.


Responsibilities


	Carry out inspection on equipment as contained in the maintenance plan, document observation accordingly.
	Check fuel, oil and cooling water level for all generator sets before startup.
	Troubleshoot control panel to identify any potential issue.
	Daily dipping of ATK storage tank for generator consumption and proper documentation of stock
	Servicing of product pump strainers
	Hose connection and disconnection for vessel reception
	Confirmation of line valves functionality prior to vessel reception to ensure hitch-free operation.
	Greasing and servicing of control/line valves for easy opening and closing
	Servicing of sprinkler nozzles.
	Render assistance on any and every maintenance task.
	Work with the team regarding repair and replacement of faulty equipment when necessary.
	Ensure the use of PPE and adhere to all safety standards.


Requirements


	Minimum of B.Sc. / HND in Engineering, Sciences, Social Sciences, Administration or Humanities&nbsp;
	Minimum of three (3) years of working experience in core Maintenance role in the Oil &amp; Gas or Manufacturing Industry&nbsp;
	Trade Test is a plus.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-05",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "name": "RainOil Limited"
    
  },

Maintenance Officer / Technician Job at Rainoil Limited




        
    

        
        Rainoil Limited is an integrated downstream oil and gas company. A prominent player in the Nigerian industry, the company’s operations span across the downstream value chain i.e. Petroleum Product Storage, Haulage/Distribution and Retail Sales. Our primary products include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO) and Kerosene (DPK).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Officer / Technician

Job Location: Nigeria
Job Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for performing preventive, corrective and routine maintenance. Troubleshooting and repairs on machinery, equipment, to prevent or reduce operational downtime and ensure optimal performance and safety.


Responsibilities


	Carry out inspection on equipment as contained in the maintenance plan, document observation accordingly.
	Check fuel, oil and cooling water level for all generator sets before startup.
	Troubleshoot control panel to identify any potential issue.
	Daily dipping of ATK storage tank for generator consumption and proper documentation of stock
	Servicing of product pump strainers
	Hose connection and disconnection for vessel reception
	Confirmation of line valves functionality prior to vessel reception to ensure hitch-free operation.
	Greasing and servicing of control/line valves for easy opening and closing
	Servicing of sprinkler nozzles.
	Render assistance on any and every maintenance task.
	Work with the team regarding repair and replacement of faulty equipment when necessary.
	Ensure the use of PPE and adhere to all safety standards.


Requirements


	Minimum of B.Sc. / HND in Engineering, Sciences, Social Sciences, Administration or Humanities 
	Minimum of three (3) years of working experience in core Maintenance role in the Oil & Gas or Manufacturing Industry 
	Trade Test is a plus.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
